---
title: "Homeworks"
page-layout: full
---

## Specifications-Graded Units

The homework sequence is **8 deliverables** — seven units plus an open **Capstone** —
consolidated into connected arcs rather than isolated weekly problem sets. Every deliverable
is graded **pass / not-yet** against published specifications: no points, no partial credit,
revise-and-resubmit.

Each unit page below gives its objectives, what you'll build, prerequisites, what to submit,
and its **rubric** (the same pages are posted in **D2L**). Assignment materials — starter
code, autograder, data — are distributed via **GitHub Classroom**; your submissions live in a
private repo visible only to you and the instructor. See [Assessment](assessment.qmd) for how
the units and Capstone set your letter grade.

| Unit | Title | You'll build |
|:----:|-------|--------------|
| [U0](homeworks/unit0.html) | Tooling & Reproducibility | A working pipeline (clone → `pytest` → render → push) and the seed policy every later unit assumes. |
| [U1](homeworks/unit1.html) | Linear Models & Model Selection | One dataset, three fits: scalar least squares → matrix normal equations → cross-validated polynomial. |
| [U2a](homeworks/unit2a.html) | Probability & Estimation Foundations (I) | Poisson probabilities + derivations: the Bernoulli MLE and its Fisher-information floor. |
| [U2b](homeworks/unit2b.html) | Probability & Estimation Foundations (II) | Written-only: the matrix-calculus identities Unit 1 applies, Gaussian independence, MLE unbiasedness, Beta moments. |
| [U3](homeworks/unit3.html) | Monte Carlo & Estimators | Estimating 𝔼[f(X)] and π by sampling; O(1/√N) convergence and the curse of dimensionality. |
| [U4](homeworks/unit4.html) | Bayesian Inference, Exact & Conjugate | Predictive-variance error bars, the exact coin-game posterior, Inverse-Gamma conjugacy. |
| [U5](homeworks/unit5.html) | Approximate Bayesian Inference | One posterior four ways: PGM, MAP (Newton), Laplace, MCMC — and why mode ≠ mean. |
| [Capstone](homeworks/capstone.html) | Open Bayesian Modelling with Uncertainty | Your model + a valid uncertainty method (BLR / Laplace / MCMC) on a new dataset, with a results-and-ethics report. |

## How the units connect

The sequence spirals rather than resets: **U0**'s seed policy is graded again in U3 and U5 ·
**U2b proves** the matrix-calculus identities **U1 applies** · U2b's Beta moments power
**U4**'s conjugate priors, and its Hessian identity returns in **U5** · U4's exact posteriors
become the targets **U5 approximates** · the Capstone's method menu *is* U4 + U5.
